# Transcrição: If_You_Want_A_Better_Sounding_Voice,_Do_THIS_Instead.

## [00:00 - 02:45]

Getting this one word wrong actually makes you sound terrible when you talk. Let me explain. First I've got a couple of questions for you. Number one, what do you do with a hammer? Well, you use a hammer to hammer things. Second question, what do you do with a drill? Well, you can use a drill to drill things. I know these questions seem rather obvious, but let me ask you a couple more. When you think about tools, what do you do with tools in general, regardless of the tool? Well, you use them. And that right there is where the problem lies. People, when they think about their voice, they think about their voice as being a tool. I know my students think of their voice as being a tool because when I ask them, what do you do with your voice? What they say is, then will we use our voice every day to communicate? And again, that right there is the biggest problem and the biggest mistake in the world of communication. Let's dive deeper into this. With a guitar, what do you do with it? Do you use a guitar? No, you play the guitar. What about a piano? Do you use a piano? No. As I look to another angle to make things more dramatic and engaging. No, you don't use a piano. You play the piano. See where I'm going with this? The key word that people get wrong when they communicate is instead of playing with their voice, they use their voice. Your voice is not a tool. It's an instrument. And if you go through this life just using your instrument, you're not being as influential nor impactful as you can be. Let me show you a clip of someone using the piano. Now let me show you someone who plays with the piano masterfully. Wasn't the difference between those two examples absolutely outrageous? The same goes for the piano. The same goes with your voice. I could use my voice. Let me give you 10 seconds of me using my voice. Alright, I'm just going to give you an example of me using my voice. And this is just me using it. I know it's a very dramatic example of it, but I'm doing this to prove a point. Sorry. Can you see, hear and feel the difference? If you learn how to play with your voice masterfully, you'll be able to move people. You'll be able to connect with people more easily, and you'll be able to have more impact every single time you open your mouth and you talk. If you want to just stop using your voice and learn how to be more playful with your voice, then join me for my final free masterclass for 2023. I've put the link in the comments. Click the link to register. Can't wait to help you amplify the best parts of who you are. See you at the masterclass.
